title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Language Clinic: Front Matter Boundary
summary: The build relies on locale encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, parses arbitrary YAML objects, accepts duplicate metadata keys, and compares dates in mixed formats. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the front matter boundary increment in versioned decoded-document and metadata contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.
Choose normalization, identifier, comparison, sorting, and display policies while retaining original author text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_text_encoding_metadata_3(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-front-matter-boundary-tvrrr3
# Competency: ssg-front-matter-boundary.
# Case 6380: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_front_matter_boundary_tvrrr3(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_tvrrr3 = "tvrrr3-3rrrvt-6380"
    assert evidence_variant_tvrrr3
    evidence_axes_tvrrr3 = (
        "readertvrrr3",
        "source6380",
        "parse3rrrvt",
        "routetvrrr36380",
        "artifact6380tvrrr3",
        "recovery3rrrvt6380",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_tvrrr3)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Front Matter Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 8917 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
